channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TARURA invests in road improvements to support key agricultural zones
item.description: The Tanzania Rural and Urban Roads Agency (TARURA) has started upgrading road networks in regions that are major producers of avocados, tea, potatoes, and wheat. These improvements aim to facilitate s...

item.title: Green Belt Movement Warns Against Forest Infrastructure Expansion
item.description: Nairobi — The Green Belt Movement (GBM) has raised serious concerns over proposed changes to Kenya's Forest Conservation and Management Act, specifically Section 56(2). The amendment seeks to give the...

item.title: Pakistan's GDP and Per Capita Income Show Gradual Economic Recovery
item.description: ISLAMABAD: According to the government, Pakistan’s economy is showing modest recovery as GDP and per capita income have increased slightly in dollar terms during the current fiscal year. The country’s...

item.title: B.C. man receives house arrest for defrauding client out of $155,800 due to gambling addiction
item.description: A British Columbia judge has sentenced Kenneth David Derksen to two years less a day of house arrest for defrauding an extremely vulnerable client of $155,800, money he spent on online gambling. Derks...

item.title: IMF Projects 5.9% Growth for Tanzania Amid Global Economic Pressures
item.description: According to the International Monetary Fund (IMF), Tanzania’s economy is showing strong resilience even with global shocks from the Middle East conflict affecting oil prices and supply chains. The IM...

item.title: Flydubai Suspends Operations to Islamabad, Lahore and Peshawar Till October Over Operational Reasons
item.description: Flydubai, the low-cost carrier from the United Arab Emirates, has announced suspension of its flights from and to Islamabad, Lahore and Peshawar till 26th October due to operational issues. However, s...

item.title: Russia Successfully Tests Sarmat Nuclear Missile, Putin Confirms Deployment Plans
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has officially welcomed the test launch of the new strategic nuclear missile, Sarmat, calling it the most powerful in the world. According to state television, Sergei ...

item.title: Tomorrow, When the War Began: Exploring Teen Life and Resilience in a Time of Crisis
item.description: Tomorrow, When the War Began, both the 2010 film and the John Marsden book series it adapts, is more than just an action-adventure story. Following a group of Aussie teens who return from a camping tr...

item.title: Labor’s modest tax changes target older generations without major impact on housing
item.description: The recent tax changes announced by the Labor government have caused quite a stir in right-wing media, but in reality, they represent incremental adjustments rather than a sweeping reform. The modific...

item.title: Teacher denies allegations of baby murder and vows legal battle
item.description: A teacher has been accused of the murder of a baby and has reportedly told the police that they will fight the charges until the day they die. Details surrounding the case, including the circumstances...

item.title: Analysis Shows Reading Skills Declining Nationwide, With L.A. and Select California Districts Making Gains
item.description: A new national study shows that U.S. students are experiencing a long-term decline in reading skills, a trend that started well before the pandemic. Harvard, Stanford, and Dartmouth researchers analyz...

item.title: Hwy. 12 in Tay Township temporarily closed following overnight collision
item.description: Early this morning, a traffic collision on Highway 12 in Tay Township led to the temporary closure of a stretch of the highway. The incident, which occurred overnight, involved at least one vehicle an...

item.title: Discussion on the Future of the New People’s Army in the Philippines
item.description: In a new episode of Rappler's program 'In the Public Square,' columnist John Nery discusses with Eddie Quitoriano, author of 'Deeper Ground, Darker Shadows,' the current situation and future prospects...

item.title: The Growing Importance of Simplicity in Financial Trading
item.description: In the ever-evolving world of trading, complexity is increasingly being replaced by simplicity as the key to success. Tinus Rautenbach, Business Head at Clarity, highlights that traders and investors ...

item.title: TNT Tropang Giga, led by Bol, overcome NLEX to keep playoff hopes alive
item.description: In a thrilling PBA matchup, TNT Tropang Giga managed to fend off the top-ranked NLEX Road Warriors, keeping their hopes alive in the Commissioner's Cup playoffs. The team, spearheaded by standout play...

item.title: JCB to Attempt World Hydrogen Land Speed Record with Hydromax Vehicle
item.description: British engineering firm JCB is gearing up to challenge the world hydrogen land speed record with its new Hydromax vehicle at the Bonneville Salt Flats in the US. This ambitious project comes 20 years...

item.title: Princess of Wales begins Italian visit to promote early childhood development following cancer recovery
item.description: The Princess of Wales, Catherine, has commenced her first major overseas tour since recovering from cancer, arriving in Reggio Emilia, Italy, today. Her visit, spanning two days, focuses on early chil...

item.title: Mandodari’s unwavering love for Ravana in the epic Ramayana
item.description: The article is an excerpt from Bulbul Sharma’s book 'Epic Love Stories' and narrates the poignant tale of Mandodari, the chief queen of Lanka. Born as an apsara named Madhura, she faced a curse that t...

item.title: EAC Kicks Off Ushirikiano Imara 2026 Security Drill to Boost Regional Cooperation
item.description: Kenya is hosting the 14th East African Community (EAC) Armed Forces Command Post Exercise, known as Ushirikiano Imara 2026, at the Humanitarian Peace Support School in Nairobi. This exercise has broug...

item.title: NDC Presidental Ticket Zoned to South to Promote Fairness and Unity, Says Party Leader
item.description: Hon. Uche Ben Odunzeh, wey dey contest for Imo West Senatorial District under the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), don yan say di party decision to zone di presidential ticket go di South dey promot...
